title: How Alice & Bob Uses NVIDIA CUDA-Q to Accelerate Quantum Error Correction Research - Alice & Bob
why:
- Alice & Bob used NVIDIA CUDA-Q to accelerate simulations of quantum error correction for their cat-qubit QPUs, specifically for Elevator Codes.
- GPU acceleration (using CUDA-Q) enabled a 9.25x speedup in decoding simulations compared to a CPU-only approach (1 hour 57 minutes vs 18 hours 2 minutes for 100,000 simulated shots).
- Faster decoding cycles allow for more aggressive iteration on code design and exploration of architectural variations in quantum error correction research.
- Elevator Codes, designed for Alice & Bob’s cat qubits, reach very low logical error rates needed for large-scale quantum algorithms and quantum chemistry simulations.
- The tight integration of classical (GPU/CPU) and quantum hardware, enabled by platforms like CUDA-Q, is helping to advance the path toward scalable, fault-tolerant quantum computing.
